options.A: It is open only to students majoring in English.
options.B: It engages with literature from a variety of genres.
options.C: It requires prior coursework in literary studies.

options.A: They will be conducted both online and in person.
options.B: They will involve group and individual assignments.
options.C: They will include attending plays and poetry readings.

options.A: To announce the opening of new university offices on North Avenue
options.B: To provide information about construction work on North Avenue
options.C: To announce the addition of new university parking garages
options.D: To promote the university's enhanced North Avenue shuttle service

options.C: Some places to park cars will be unavailable.

options.B: Announcing a disruption to a service

options.D: Promoting a community water park opening

questions.question: What should residents do beforehand?

options.C: Inspect faucets and showers for leaks

options.D: It is due to a natural disaster.

paragraphs: The demographic transition model (DTM) illustrates the shifting patterns of birth and death rates as societies evolve from agrarian to industrialized economies. Initially, both rates are high, resulting in slow population growth due to limited access to healthcare and sanitation. As countries develop, death rates decline sharply with advances in medical care and improved living conditions. Yet, birth rates often remain high, spurring rapid population growth that can strain resources. This transition highlights the tension between demographic momentum and societal capability to meet the growing needs.

A further decline in birth rates eventually occurs, influenced by greater access to education and changes in cultural norms. Families begin to view fewer children as advantageous, aligning with shifts toward economic and personal aspirations. However, this stage brings its own challenges, including the potential for an aging population and workforce shortages.

Some demographers postulate a fifth stage, characterized by persistently low birth rates that might lead to population contraction. The implications of this are complex, ranging from economic innovation driven by necessity to increased strain on social institutions and intergenerational support systems. Understanding these transitions enables societies to better plan for future demographic realities, though the dynamic interplay of these factors is still unfolding.

questions.question: According to paragraph 1, what does the demographic transition model primarily demonstrate?

options.A: Decreasing birth rates as societies industrialize
options.B: Changing rates of birth and death as economies develop
options.C: Differences in healthcare access among societies
options.D: Consequences of rising death rates in industrial societies

questions.question: Which of the following can be inferred about agrarian societies?

options.A: They are more prosperous than industrialized societies.
options.B: They are an exception to the trends proposed by the demographic transition model.
options.C: They experience high birth rates and also high death rates.
options.D: They suffer sharp population declines as a result of high death rates.

questions.question: The word "aspirations" in the passage is closest in meaning to
questions.special_content: Families begin to view fewer children as advantageous, aligning with shifts toward economic and personal <mark>aspirations</mark>.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned about low birth rates?

options.A: Sustained low birth rates result in negative population growth.
options.B: Demographic changes brought about by low birth rates can lead to societal tensions.
options.C: Persistently low birth rates typically lead to economic decline.
options.D: Understanding demographic transitions linked to low birth rates helps societies plan for the future.

questions.question:
There are four locations [■] in the passage that indicate where the following sentence could be added.

As societies navigate this demographic shift, proactive policies and innovations become essential to sustain economic growth and promote well-being among older adults.

Where would the sentence best fit? Select a location [■] to add the sentence to the passage.
questions.special_content:
...aligning with shifts toward economic and personal aspirations. [■] However, this stage brings its own challenges, including the potential for an aging population and workforce shortages. [■]
Some demographers postulate a fifth stage, characterized by persistently low birth rates that might lead to population contraction. [■] The implications of this are complex, ranging from economic innovation driven by necessity to increased strain on social institutions and intergenerational support systems. [■]
questions.inserted_sentence: As societies navigate this demographic shift, proactive policies and innovations become essential to sustain economic growth and promote well-being among older adults.

paragraphs: Glaciers, repositories of ancient ice, serve as frozen libraries of Earth's climatic history. As snow accumulates and compresses over centuries, it traps air bubbles that create tiny atmospheric snapshots. These bubbles are crucial for understanding climate evolution. By analyzing the gases within the bubbles, researchers are able to reconstruct past concentrations of greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide and methane, revealing both natural climate cycles and the escalating impact of human activity.

Though many glaciers are retreating due to rising temperatures, surviving ones offer a window into the past. Scientists drill deep into the ice to extract cylindrical cores, whose layers, much like tree rings, correspond to distinct time periods. These layers contain more than just gases: They hold chemical and physical markers of past environmental events. Ice cores can reveal data about volcanic eruptions via ash and sulfate layers, solar activity via isotopic changes, and forest fires via trapped particulates and black carbon.

Recent studies underscore the precarious future of these icy archives. As glaciers melt, they contribute to rising sea levels, and the risk that invaluable climate data will be lost increases. The urgency to study and preserve these records grows. While scientists race against time, the question remains: what secrets do these frozen giants still hold?

questions.question: Identify the sentence in paragraph 1 that explains the natural process by which climate data is preserved.
questions.answer: As snow accumulates and compresses over centuries, it traps air bubbles that create tiny atmospheric snapshots.

questions.question: The word "escalating" in the passage is closest in meaning to
questions.special_content: By analyzing the gases within the bubbles, researchers are able to reconstruct past concentrations of greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide and methane, revealing both natural climate cycles and the <mark>escalating</mark> impact of human activity.

questions.question: According to the passage, all of the following are true about ice cores EXCEPT:

options.A: Ice cores are obtained by drilling far down into glaciers.
options.B: Each layer in an ice core formed at a different point in time.
options.C: Ice core layers are sometimes destroyed by severe environmental events.
options.D: Ice cores can be used to identify when volcanoes erupted in the past.

questions.question: Why does the author mention "tree rings"?

options.A: To compare the usefulness of trees and ice cores for studying past climates
options.B: To highlight the extreme age of some layers of ice cores
options.C: To illustrate the layering that exists in ice cores
options.D: To contrast the physical markers present in tree rings with the chemical markers found in ice cores

questions.special_content: Scientists drill deep into the ice to extract cylindrical cores, whose layers, much like <mark>tree rings</mark>, correspond to distinct time periods.

questions.question: The passage implies that scientists should

options.A: look quickly for other sources of valuable climate data
options.B: increase the size of the ice cores they drill
options.C: find ways to limit the rise of sea levels
options.D: work as quickly as possible to collect more ice cores
